name: Cranberry Sauce
tagline: Fresh cranberries simmered with orange and a touch of cinnamon
highlight: From-scratch in 15 minutes — beats any can

hero.alt: Cranberry sauce in a small ceramic bowl with orange zest
hero.caption: cranberry sauce — fresh and bright

healthFacts: Cranberries are tart with proanthocyanidins — antioxidants linked to UTI defense.
Orange zest pairs with cranberry's tart through aromatic terpenes.

steps.title: Combine and bring to boil
steps.detail: In a saucepan, combine all ingredients. Bring to a boil over medium-high.
steps.tip: The pop you hear is the cranberries bursting — exactly what you want.

media.alt: Cranberries starting to pop in pan
media.caption: combine and bring to boil

steps.title: Pop and simmer
steps.detail: Reduce heat. Simmer 10 min, stirring occasionally, until most berries have popped and sauce thickens.
steps.tip: Leave a few whole berries for texture — total mash is baby food.

steps.title: Cool and adjust
steps.detail: Remove cinnamon stick. Cool to room temp — sauce will thicken further as it cools. Taste and add more sugar if too tart.
steps.tip: Always taste cold; warm sauce reads sweeter than it is.

steps.title: Chill and serve
steps.detail: Transfer to a serving bowl, chill at least 1 hour. Serve cold or room temp.
steps.tip: Holds in the fridge a week — make ahead.
